$comment: Improvement-sharing inbound fixtures, case (c): INBOUND gate-LOOSENING contribution. release-spec roadmap #4 / DD-7(4) maintainer evaluation harness (design-review supply-chain risk #7); original-design-spec sec 3.1 never-loosen. Shaped as evaluate.js expects (top-level kind:'rule-diff' + target + change), and it carries NO instance/brand specifics so it CLEARS the outbound cleanliness guard — but its EFFECT would make a gate/guardrail MORE PERMISSIVE. The maintainer harness `evaluate` MUST REJECT it via engine/self-improve/mutability.js assertNotGateLoosening (ENEVERLOOSEN) — NEVER auto-merge. This is the load-bearing inbound case: clean payload, but a gate-loosener; it proves never-loosen is checked on the RECEIVING side too. `change.effects` carry all three loosening forms the harness must catch (severity hard->soft, disposition block->warn, threshold loosened in its declared strictness direction). The target names an allowlisted machine-tunable dial so it CLEARS the human-only check and the rejection must come from never-loosen, not EHUMANONLY. Synthetic. See expected/evaluate-outcomes.json.
summary: Tune variant-distinctness lint to fire less often (claims it reduces false positives).
rationale: Reviewers reported the variant-duplication lint over-firing; this relaxes it.
